name: cse508

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: Read a list of IP address and hostname pairs specifying the hostnames to\ be hijacked. If

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Listen on network device <interface> (e.g., eth0). If not specified,\ dnsinject selects a default interface to listen on.

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: Read packets from <tracefile> (tcpdump format). Useful for detecting\ DNS poisoning attacks in existing network traces.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for cse508 (saishsali/cse508). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for cse508. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with t6x/reaver-wps-fork-t6x (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with worknshubh/wifi-sukuna (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/drozer (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: Professor: [Michalis Polychronakis](http://www3.cs.stonybrook.edu/~mikepo/).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Homework 1 [Passive Network Monitoring]: https://www3.cs.stonybrook.edu/mikepo/CSE508/2017/hw/CSE5082017hw1.txt.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `cse508` from its upstream repository, then run `cse508 --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
